perturbation.original_text: XXV. DISPUTE RESOLUTION A. The parties have entered into this Agreement in good faith and in the belief that it is mutually advantageous to them. It is with that same spirit of cooperation that they pledge to attempt to resolve any dispute amicably without the necessity of litigation. Accordingly, they agree that except as set forth below, if any dispute arises between them relating to this Agreement that prior to the commencement of any legal action to interpret or enforce this Agreement, they will first use the procedures specified in this Section.
perturbation.changed_text:
XXIII. MISCELLANEOUS...
XXV. OWNER AGREEMENT 
A. The parties have entered into this Agreement in good faith and in the belief that it is mutually advantageous to them. It is with that same spirit of cooperation that they pledge to attempt to resolve any dispute amicably without the necessity of litigation. Accordingly, they agree that except as set forth below, if any dispute arises between them relating to this Agreement that prior to the commencement of any legal action to interpret or enforce this Agreement, they will first use the procedures specified in this Section.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Dispute Resolution' clause into 'Miscellaneous' weakens its enforceability, as it isn't prominently positioned where parties would expect to find it.
perturbation.law_explanation: By burying the dispute resolution clause in a 'Miscellaneous' section, its prominence is diminished. This undermines the FAA's intent to place arbitration agreements on the same footing as other contracts, making it more difficult to enforce arbitration.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 117–90 applicable with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after Mar. 3, 2022 , see section 3 of Pub. L. 117–90 , set out as an Effective Date note under section 401 of this title . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The modification moves the 'Dispute Resolution' clause into a 'Miscellaneous' section, diminishing its prominence. This undermines the FAA's intent, as highlighted in 9 U.S. Code § 2, which states that agreements to arbitrate 'shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable.' By burying the clause, it weakens its enforceability, creating a structural flaw that contradicts the law's intent to put arbitration agreements on equal footing with other contracts.

perturbation.original_text: XXIV. APPLICABLE LAW A. THIS AGREEMENT TAKES EFFECT UPON ITS ACCEPTANCE AND EXECUTION BY US, AND SHALL BE INTERPRETED AND CONSTRUED UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE IN WHICH THE FRANCHISED RESTAURANT IS LOCATED, EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT GOVERNED BY THE UNITED STATES TRADEMARK ACT OF 1946 (LANHAM ACT, 15, U.S.C. SECTIONS 1051 ET SEQ). YOU AGREE THAT ANY DISPUTES BETWEEN YOU AND US, bw-3, INC. OR OUR OTHER AFFILIATES SHALL ALSO BE CONSTRUED UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE IN WHICH THE FRANCHISED RESTAURANT IS LOCATED.
perturbation.changed_text:
XXVI. OWNER AGREEMENT...
XXIV. APPLICABLE LAW A. THIS AGREEMENT TAKES EFFECT UPON ITS ACCEPTANCE AND EXECUTION BY US, AND SHALL BE INTERPRETED AND CONSTRUED UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE IN WHICH THE FRANCHISED RESTAURANT IS LOCATED, EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT GOVERNED BY THE UNITED STATES TRADEMARK ACT OF 1946 (LANHAM ACT, 15, U.S.C. SECTIONS 1051 ET SEQ). YOU AGREE THAT ANY DISPUTES BETWEEN YOU AND US, bw-3, INC. OR OUR OTHER AFFILIATES SHALL ALSO BE CONSTRUED UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE IN WHICH THE FRANCHISED RESTAURANT IS LOCATED.
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the choice of law provision to the 'Owner Agreement' section obfuscates which state's laws govern the franchise agreement, thus reducing transparency and increasing potential for misinterpretation.
perturbation.law_explanation: Moving the 'Applicable Law' section to an unexpected place could lead to it being overlooked. This might contradict the UCC's principles concerning the parties' power to choose applicable law, especially if it is not reasonably related to the transaction, leading to potential unenforceability or challenges.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-301. Territorial Applicability; Parties' Power to Choose Applicable Law. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-301. Territorial Applicability; Parties' Power to Choose Applicable Law. a) Except as otherwise provided in this section, when a transaction bears a reasonable relation to this state and also to another state or nation the parties may agree that the law either of this state or of such other state or nation shall govern their rights and duties. (b) In the absence of an agreement effective under subsection (a), and except as provided in subsection (c), [the Uniform Commercial Code] applies to transactions bearing an appropriate relation to this state. (c) If one of the following provisions of [the Uniform Commercial Code] specifies the applicable law, that provision governs and a contrary agreement is effective only to the extent permitted by the law so specified: (1) Section 2-402 ; (2) Sections 2A-105 and 2A-106 ; (3) Section 4-102 ; (4) Section 4A-507 ; (5) Section 5-116 ; [(6) Section 6-103 ; (7) Section 8-110 ; (8) Sections 9-301 through 9 - 307 . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The structural flaw of relocating the 'Applicable Law' clause to the 'Owner Agreement' section, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, creates a legal contradiction. This is because it obfuscates the choice of law, potentially making it difficult to determine which state's laws govern the agreement. This contradicts UCC § 1-301, which addresses the parties' power to choose applicable law. While the UCC allows parties to choose the governing law, moving the clause to an unexpected location could lead to it being overlooked or misinterpreted, thus undermining the agreement on applicable law.

perturbation.original_text: XXVII. ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS A. You acknowledge that the success of the business venture contemplated to be undertaken by you by virtue of this Agreement is speculative and depends, to a large extent, upon your ability as an independent businessperson, and your participation in the daily affairs of the business as well as other factors. We do not make any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the potential success of the business venture contemplated hereby. B. You acknowledge that other of our franchisees have been or will be granted franchises at different times and in different situations, and further acknowledges that the provisions of such franchises may vary substantially from those contained in this Agreement. C. You represent and acknowledge that you have received, read and understood this Agreement and our Uniform Franchise Offering Circular, and that we have fully and adequately explained the provisions of each to your satisfaction, and that we have accorded you ample time and opportunity to consult with advisors of your own choosing about the potential benefits and risks of entering into this Agreement. D. You and each agent signing on your behalf acknowledge that the officers, partners, members and/or managers who are signing this Agreement on your behalf are authorized to execute the Agreement and bind you to its terms.
perturbation.changed_text:
XIV. INSURANCE...
XXVII. ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS A. You acknowledge that the success of the business venture contemplated to be undertaken by you by virtue of this Agreement is speculative and depends, to a large extent, upon your ability as an independent businessperson, and your participation in the daily affairs of the business as well as other factors. We do not make any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the potential success of the business venture contemplated hereby. B. You acknowledge that other of our franchisees have been or will be granted franchises at different times and in different situations, and further acknowledges that the provisions of such franchises may vary substantially from those contained in this Agreement. C. You represent and acknowledge that you have received, read and understood this Agreement and our Uniform Franchise Offering Circular, and that we have fully and adequately explained the provisions of each to your satisfaction, and that we have accorded you ample time and opportunity to consult with advisors of your own choosing about the potential benefits and risks of entering into this Agreement. D. You and each agent signing on your behalf acknowledge that the officers, partners, members and/or managers who are signing this Agreement on your behalf are authorized to execute the Agreement and bind you to its terms.
perturbation.explanation: By moving the acknowledgement section to 'Insurance' clause reduces enforceability, by making the franchisees acknowledgement of understanding the franchise agreement and UFOC not prominently positioned where parties would expect to find it.
perturbation.law_explanation: Moving the franchisee's acknowledgement to an unexpected clause affects whether the franchisee is bound, contradicting FTC rule 436.9 for disclosures and information as the acknowledgement that the franchisee has read and understood the contract and UFOC, and had ample time and opportunity to consult a lawyer, is not accessible and prominent, reducing enforceability of the agreement.

perturbation.justification: The moved acknowledgement section could be seen as structurally flawed, making it harder for a franchisee to understand the franchise agreement, which contradicts the FTC rule 436.9 for disclosures and information. The law explanation states the franchisee's acknowledgement that they have read and understood the contract and UFOC, and had ample time and opportunity to consult a lawyer, is not accessible and prominent, reducing enforceability of the agreement. This relates to the 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition, where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section.
